title: LEGO
content:
{{Top|rwc}} {{RealCompany |image=[[File:Lego.svg]] |name=The LEGO Group
{{C|LEGO System A/S}} |founded=[[1930s|1932]]{{LEGOWebCite|url=en-us/aboutus/lego-group/the-lego-group-history|text=The LEGO Group History}} |founder=Ole Kirk Kristiansen |dissolved= |headquarters=[[Wikipedia:Billund, Denmark|Billund]], [[Wikipedia:Denmark|Denmark]] |parent= |subsid= |website=[http://www.lego.com LEGO's official website] }} {{Quote|Do that so fast, how did you?''"
"''We're kids. Kids are good at this stuff.|[[Yoda]] and [[Vaash Ti]], on building with LEGO|{{TheYodaChronicles|The Phantom Clone}}}} '''The LEGO Group''' is a company that produces '''LEGO''' branded toys. Although [[LEGO Star Wars|LEGO ''Star Wars'']] is non-canon, products and stories have tied in to material from both the former [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] and new [[canon]] continuities, such as adapting or parodying the events and characters. ==Development== [[File:Lego_AT-AT.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A LEGO Star Wars Imperial AT-AT Walker]] The LEGO brand of plastic toy bricks is produced by LEGO System A/S, better known as the LEGO Group, which was founded in [[1930s|1932]] by Ole Kirk Kristiansen and is headquartered in [[Wikipedia:Billund, Denmark|Billund]], [[Wikipedia:Denmark|Denmark]]. Beginning in [[1999]], the LEGO Group began releasing LEGO ''[[Star Wars]]'' products as their first licensed property. The LEGO ''Star Wars'' construction sets and [[Wikipedia:Lego minifigure|minifigures]] have included characters, locations, and vehicles. Further tie-ies include books, short films, television series, and [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s.{{WebCite|url=https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lego-group-honors-20-years-of-lego-star-wars-with-guinness-world-records-record-breaking-build-to-open-star-wars-celebration-300831213.html|text=LEGO Group Honors 20 Years of LEGO® Star Wars™ with GUINNESS WORLD RECORDS™ Record-Breaking Build to Open Star Wars Celebration|author=The LEGO Group|date=2019-04-19|archivedate=20190421081738}} Jens Kronvold Frederiksen, who is LEGO's design director for ''Star Wars'', has been involved with LEGO ''Stars Wars'' since [[1998]].{{SW|url=news/20-years-of-lego-star-wars-design-director-jens-kronvold-frederiksen|text=20 Years of LEGO ''Star Wars'': Design Director Jens Kronvold Frederiksen Looks Back}} He frequently collaborates with [[Chris Gollaher]], [[Lucasfilm]]'s director of product development.{{SW|url=news/swcc-2019-6-things-we-learned-from-the-lego-star-wars-20th-anniversary-panel|text=SWCC 2019: 6 Things We Learned from the LEGO ''Star Wars'' 20th Anniversary Panel}} The LEGO ''Star Wars'' short films and television series have included adaptations of the feature films along with original stories. They have been broadcast on [[Cartoon Network]],{{SW|url=news/announcing-lego-star-wars-the-yoda-chronicles|text=Announcing LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles}} [[Disney XD]], and [[Disney+]].{{SW|url=news/10-essential-episodes-of-lego-star-wars-to-watch-on-disney|text=10 Essential Episodes of LEGO ''Star Wars'' to Watch on Disney+}} The animation for the [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s is produced by [[TT Games]]. Earlier releases did not have character dialogue. ==Media== Beyond the ''Star Wars'' theme itself, there are ''Star Wars'' references in two ''LEGO Indiana Jones'' sets; [[w:c:brickipedia:7621 The Lost Tomb|7621 The Lost Tomb]],{{LEGOWebCite|url=cdn/product-assets/product.bi.core.pdf/4527304.pdf|text=Building instructions for 7621, Indiana Jones™ and the Lost Tomb, LEGO® Indiana Jones™}} and [[w:c:brickipedia:77013 Escape from the Lost Tomb|77013 Escape from the Lost Tomb]], with both sets depicting [[C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2]] in hieroglyphs.{{LEGOWebCite|url=en-us/product/escape-from-the-lost-tomb-77013|text=Escape from the Lost Tomb}} ===Books=== ====Film adaptations==== Young-readers film adaptations, ''LEGO Star Wars''-style. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Save the Galaxy!]]'' (2011) — Young-readers adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]; published by Scholastic. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Phantom Menace]]'' (2012) — DK Readers Level 2. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Attack of the Clones]]'' (2013) — DK Readers Level 2. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith]]'' (2013) — DK Readers Level 3. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: A New Hope]]'' (2014) — DK Readers Level 1. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' (2014) — DK Readers Level 2. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Return of the Jedi]]'' (2014) — DK Readers Level 3. *[[LEGO Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (Scholastic)|''LEGO Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith'']] (2015) — Scholastic. *[[LEGO Star Wars: A New Hope (Scholastic)|''LEGO Star Wars: A New Hope'']] (2015) — Scholastic. *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens (DK Readers)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens'']] (2016) — DK Readers Level 2. *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens (Scholastic)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens'']] (2016) — Scholastic. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Last Jedi]]'' (2018) — DK Readers Level 2. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker]]'' (2020) — DK Readers Level 2. ====''LEGO Star Wars'' stories==== *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Anakin: Space Pilot]]'' (2011) — Young-readers story of Anakin Skywalker's piloting achievements from childhood through the Empire. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Darth Maul's Mission]]'' (2012) — Young-readers adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] from Darth Maul's perspective. *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Padawan Menace (book)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Padawan Menace'']] (2012) — Young-readers adaptation of the short film ''LEGO Star Wars: The Padawan Menace''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Anakin to the Rescue]]'' (2012) — Follow-up to ''LEGO Star Wars: Darth Maul's Mission''; young-readers adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Out (book)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Out'']] (2013) — Young-readers adaptation of the TV special ''LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Out''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Brickmaster: Battle for the Stolen Crystals]]'' (2013) — Four-part adventure story, plus bricks and minifigures. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Yoda's Secret Missions]]'' (2014) — Reference materials about the Jedi Order and three stories: "The Phantom Podracer," "Home, Swamp Home," and "Jedi Temple of Doom." *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Vader Chronicles]]'' (2015) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Vader's Secret Missions]]'' (2015) — Reference material about the Sith and three stories: "The Emperor's New Clones," "Every Darth Has His Day," and "Who's Afraid of the Big Bad Sith?" *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Droid Tales Episodes I-III]]'' (2015) — Young-readers adaptation of the first three ''LEGO Star Wars: Droid Tales'' episodes from C-3PO and R2-D2's perspectives. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Tales of the Rebellion]]'' (2016) — Young-readers story collection starring C-3PO. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: R2-D2 to the Rescue!]]'' (2016) — Young-readers story collection starring R2-D2. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Great Adventures]]'' (2018) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Choose Your Path]]'' (2018) — Adventure book with 30 possible endings. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Brick Adventures: Awesome Jedi Tales]]'' (2019) — Compilation of the following: **''[[LEGO Star Wars: Brick Adventures 1: Stormtrooper Class Clowns]]'' (2018) — Three stories: "First Day at the First Order," "Captain Phasma's Wild Ride," and "The Best Worst Villain." **''[[LEGO Star Wars: Brick Adventures 2: Jedi Master Class]]'' (2020) — Three stories: "Luke And His Rey of Sunshine," "Dengar-3PO," and "Now, This is Podracing!" ====Reference and activity books==== *''[[Ultimate Sticker Collection: LEGO Star Wars: Minifigures]]'' (2012) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles (book)]]'' (2013) — Focuses on the minifigure Yoda as seen in ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles]]''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Dark Side]]'' (2014) — Focuses on minifigures aligned with the dark side. *''[[LEGO Star Wars in 100 Scenes]]'' (2015) — Follow-up to ''[[Star Wars in 100 Scenes]]'' with LEGO products. *''[[Ultimate Sticker Collection: LEGO Star Wars: Mighty Minifigures]]'' (2015) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Into Battle]]'' (2015) — Duels and battles. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Free the Galaxy]]'' (2015) — Focuses on minifigures in the Rebel Alliance. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Power of the Jedi]]'' (2015) — Activity book and companion to ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Power of the Sith]]''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Small Scenes from a Big Galaxy]]'' (2015) — Photography book. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Dooku's Army]]'' (2015) — Focuses on minifigures aligned with the Separatists or otherwise against the Republic. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Rogues and Villains]]'' (2015) — Focuses on minifigures, such as Hutts, bounty hunters, and pirates. *''[[Ultimate Factivity Collection: LEGO Star Wars]]'' (2016) — Reference and activities including drawing, quizzes, and stickers. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Chronicles of the Force]]'' (2016) — Follows the format of ''LEGO Star Wars: The Dark Side'' and ''LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Quest for the Kyber Saber]]'' (2016) — Activity book based on ''LEGO Star Wars: The Freemaker Adventures''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Official Stormtrooper Training Manual]]'' (2016) — Activity book and reference presented as an in-universe field guide for new stormtroopers. *''[[The Amazing Book of LEGO Star Wars]]'' (2017) *''[[Ultimate LEGO Star Wars]]'' (2017) — Guide to sets and minifigures from 1999 to 2017. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Face Off]]'' (2018) — Head-to-head minifigure matchups. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Ideas Book: More than 200 Games, Activities, and Building Ideas]]'' (2018) — Activity book with 200 imaginative play and building ideas. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Official Force Training Manual]]'' (2018) — Activity and guidebook to the Force, the Sith, and the Jedi. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Build Your Own Adventure: Galactic Missions]]'' (2019) — Story starters with building ideas; includes a minifigure and bricks. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Holiday Sticker Book]]'' (2020) — Sticker book with a winter holiday theme. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Yoda's Galaxy Atlas]]'' (2021) ====Strategy guides==== *''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2006) — Strategy guide for the video game ''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2006) — Strategy guide for the video game ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2007) — Strategy guide for the video game ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]''. *''[[LEGO Star Wars III: The Clone Wars: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' (2011) — Strategy guide for the video game ''[[LEGO Star Wars III: The Clone Wars]]''. ====Visual dictionaries and character encyclopedias==== *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary]]'' (2009) — Guide to sets and minifigures from 1999 to 2009. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Character Encyclopedia]]'' (2011) — Reference for over 300 minifigures. *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary: Updated and Expanded]]'' (2014) *''[[LEGO Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]'' (2015) — Update and expansion of the 2011 book. *''[[LEGO Star Wars Visual Dictionary, New Edition]]'' (2019) ===Short films=== *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Han Solo Affair]]'' (2002) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Revenge of the Brick]]'' (2005) *[[LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2 (film)|''LEGO Star Wars: The Quest for R2-D2'']] (2009) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: Bombad Bounty]]'' (2010) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Padawan Menace]]'' (2011) *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Out]]'' (2012) *''[[The Star Wars Movie Shorts'']] (2008-2020) * ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles]]'' (2013-2014) *[[LEGO Star Wars: Microfighters (video series)|''LEGO Star Wars: Microfighters'']] (2014, 2016)
